clinical_coherence.notes: 60M with prominent early autonomic dysfunction (orthostatic hypotension, erectile dysfunction, urinary dysfunction, constipation) preceding motor parkinsonism. Symmetric bilateral parkinsonism without rest tremor, poor levodopa response (17.6%), severe autonomic failure on testing (46mmHg systolic drop, diffuse anhidrosis 62%, neurogenic bladder). Presentation is classic for MSA-P, not idiopathic PD.

tool_output_consistency.notes: DaTscan symmetric bilateral (atypical for PD), levodopa challenge poor (17.6%), autonomic testing reveals severe neurogenic orthostatic hypotension and preganglionic sudomotor failure, neuropsych shows processing speed deficit. All findings consistently point to MSA-P rather than idiopathic PD.

ground_truth_quality.notes: The ground truth primary diagnosis should be reviewed - with this clinical picture (early severe autonomic failure, symmetric parkinsonism, poor levodopa response, preganglionic sudomotor failure), MSA-P is the more likely diagnosis than idiopathic PD. The case is filed under 'parkinsons' condition but the clinical features overwhelmingly suggest MSA. If the intent is that this IS MSA masquerading as PD, the ground truth should reflect that.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Genuine diagnostic ambiguity between PD and MSA-P. Early autonomic failure is the key discriminating feature. Excellent puzzle difficulty.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 60M with HTN, BPH, chronic constipation, ED, recurrent UTIs. Realistic for either PD or MSA onset.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case for PD vs MSA differentiation. Autonomic failure severity, preganglionic vs postganglionic pattern, symmetric DaTscan, poor levodopa response, and thermoregulatory sweat testing are all high-yield learning points.

issues_found: Ground truth primary diagnosis needs verification - clinical features are much more consistent with MSA-P than idiopathic PD
Filed under 'parkinsons' condition but the case may actually be teaching MSA recognition
strengths: Comprehensive autonomic testing with preganglionic pattern
Thermoregulatory sweat test adds diagnostic specificity
